name: Arcade Interactive Demo API
what_it_does: Interactive product demo creation platform with API — enabling product marketing and sales teams to capture browser-based product screenshots and flows, add interactive hotspots and annotations, embed demos on websites and in emails, and track viewer engagement analytics.
use_cases: Querying arcade demo view analytics and engagement metrics for content performance tracking
Managing demo collections and distributing demos to sales teams via API
Integrating Arcade demo engagement data with CRM for prospect intent signals
Automating demo creation workflow from product screenshot capture to publication
Embedding analytics data in revenue operations dashboards for demo performance reporting
not_for: Live product demos and screen sharing (use Zoom or Loom for live demos)
Product walkthroughs that require actual product environment (use Navattic or Demostack)
Video screen recording without interactive elements (use Loom for video)
best_when: A product marketing or sales team wants to create lightweight, screenshot-based interactive product demos quickly for websites, email campaigns, and sales outreach without environment overhead.
avoid_when: You need fully functional demo environments (use Demostack or Reprise), real-time screen sharing (use Zoom), or video-first content (use Loom or Descript).

auth.notes: API key from Arcade account. Bearer token auth. Workspace-scoped access. No OAuth2 or webhooks. Demo embedding uses public embed codes separate from API. Analytics data queryable via API.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free: 3 demos, Arcade branding, limited analytics
pricing.notes: San Francisco-based company. Founded 2021. Raised $15M. Series A from Accel. Chrome extension for capture. Very fast demo creation (< 5 minutes per demo). Screen capture + annotation workflow. Used by 5,000+ companies. Strong in PLG and sales-assisted motion for SaaS products.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API for demos, workspaces, and analytics. Bearer token auth. Limited developer documentation. Demo creation is primarily via Chrome extension UI — API is mainly for data retrieval and management. Analytics queryable by demo ID. Embed code is a separate public URL.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Demo reads fully idempotent. Analytics reads idempotent by date range. Workspace reads idempotent. Demo collection reads idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Demo creation requires Chrome extension — API cannot create demos programmatically
No webhooks — implement polling for analytics updates
Public embed codes are separate from API access — different credential types
Free tier has Arcade branding on embeds — upgrade required for white-label
Analytics data has processing delay of several hours after demo views

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. GDPR and SOC2 compliant. US data residency. Bearer token auth. Product screenshots in demos may contain sensitive UI — review content before public publishing. Embed analytics track viewer behavior — implement consent for GDPR.
